general.g_text_audio: 실례합니다. 김영수 씨 있어요? 잠깐 만나러 왔는데요.

g_text_audio_translate.vi: Xin lỗi. Có ông Kim Youngsoo ở đây không? Tôi tới gặp ông ấy một lát.
g_text_audio_translate.en: Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.fr:
Pardon. Est-ce que M. Kim Youngsoo est ici ? Je suis venu le voir un moment.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.es:
Perdóneme. ¿Está el Sr. Kim Youngsoo aquí? Vine a verlo por un tiempo.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.id:
Permisi. Apakah Tuan Kim Youngsoo ada di sini? Aku datang menemuinya sebentar.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.ja:
すみません。キム・ヨンスさんはいますか？私はしばらく彼に会いに来ました。

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.de:
Verzeihung. Ist Herr Kim Youngsoo hier? Ich kam, um ihn für eine Weile zu sehen.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.zh-CN:
打扰一下。金英秀先生在吗？我来见他一会儿。

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.zh-TW:
打擾一下。金英秀先生在嗎？我來見他一會兒。

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.it:
Mi scusi. Il signor Kim Youngsoo è qui? Sono venuto a trovarlo per un po'.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.ru:
Прошу прощения. Господин Ким Ёнсу здесь? Я пришёл к нему на некоторое время.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.th:
ขออนุญาต. คุณคิมยองซูอยู่ที่นี่หรือเปล่า? ฉันมาพบเขาสักพักหนึ่ง

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.pt:
Com licença. O Sr. Kim Youngsoo está aqui? Vim vê-lo por um tempo.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.hi:
माफ़ करें। क्या श्री किम यंगसू यहाँ हैं? मैं थोड़ी देर के लिए उनसे मिलने आया था.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.
g_text_audio_translate.tr:
Affedersin. Bay Kim Youngsoo burada mı? Bir süreliğine onu görmeye geldim.

#######

Excuse me. Is Mr. Kim Youngsoo here? I came to see him for a while.

네, 그런데요: यह दिखाने के लिए उपयोग किया जाता है कि आप वह व्यक्ति हैं जिसके बारे में वे पूछ रहे हैं, लेकिन आप अधिक जानकारी की प्रतीक्षा कर रहे हैं (उन्होंने क्यों फोन किया, आदि)।

चरित्र यह निर्धारित कर रहा है कि क्या यह सु एमआई का घरेलू फ़ोन नंबर है।#######1. Yes, that's right.2. Yes, I know.3. Yes, here it is.4. Yes, please come in.--------------------여보세요: Hello (when calling and answering the phone)
네, 그런데요: Used to show that you are the person they’re asking for, but you’re waiting for more information (why they called, etc.).

The character is determining if this is Su Mi's home phone number.
explain.tr:
1. Evet, doğru.2. Evet biliyorum.3. Evet, işte burada.4. Evet, lütfen içeri gelin.--------------------여보세요:Merhaba (ararken ve telefonu cevaplarken)
네, 그런데요: Aradıkları kişi olduğunuzu ancak daha fazla bilgi beklediğinizi (neden aradıkları vb.) göstermek için kullanılır.

Karakter bunun Su Mi'nin ev telefonu numarası olup olmadığını belirliyor.#######1. Yes, that's right.2. Yes, I know.3. Yes, here it is.4. Yes, please come in.--------------------여보세요: Hello (when calling and answering the phone)
네, 그런데요: Used to show that you are the person they’re asking for, but you’re waiting for more information (why they called, etc.).

The character is determining if this is Su Mi's home phone number.

1. Tôi sẽ ăn thật ngon. 
2. Tôi vẫn ổn. 
3. Lâu rồi không gặp bạn. 
4. Rất vui khi gặp bạn. 
--------------------
Khi lần đầu gặp mặt, người Hàn hay dùng câu "처음 뵙겠습니다", thể hiện sự vui mừng khi lần đầu tiên được gặp nhau.
explain.en:
1. Thank you for this food.
2. I've been doing well.
3. Long time no see.
4. Nice to meet you. 
--------------------
When someone tells you <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>, you can say <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g> as "nice to meet you too".
explain.fr:
1. Merci pour cette nourriture.
2. Je vais bien.
3. Long temps sans voir.
4. Ravi de vous rencontrer.
--------------------
Quand quelqu'un te dit<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>, tu peux dire<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>comme "enchanté de vous rencontrer aussi".

#######

1. Thank you for this food.
2. I've been doing well.
3. Long time no see.
4. Nice to meet you.

2. 我一直做得很好。
3.好久不见。
4. 很高兴认识你。
--------------------
当有人告诉你<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>， 你可以说<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>作为“很高兴认识你”。

#######

1. Thank you for this food.
2. I've been doing well.
3. Long time no see.
4. Nice to meet you. 
--------------------
When someone tells you <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>, you can say <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g> as "nice to meet you too".

2. 我一直做得很好。
3.好久不見。
4. 很高興認識你。
--------------------
當有人告訴你<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>， 你可以說<g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>作為“很高興認識你”。

2. У меня все хорошо.

1. Thank you for this food.
2. I've been doing well.

4. Nice to meet you.

When someone tells you <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g>, you can say <g>"만나서 반갑습니다"</g> as "nice to meet you too".

<g>안녕히계세요:</g> Goodbye (to one who is staying)

<g>안녕히 계세요:</g>जब आप किसी से दूर जा रहे हों तो औपचारिक रूप से अलविदा
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g>औपचारिक रूप से अलविदा जब कोई आपसे दूर जा रहा हो

#######

1. Please come in.
2. Welcome.
3. Goodbye.
4. Goodbye.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g> Bye in formal when you are going away from someone
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g> Bye in formal when someone is going away from you

explain.vi:
1. Tạm biệt.
2. Chúc ngủ ngon.
3. Tôi sẽ ăn thật ngon.
4. Cảm ơn.
--------------------
안녕히 계세요: Tạm biệt (người đi nói)
안녕히 가세요: Tạm biệt (người ở lại nói)

Hai câu này dùng khi đối đáp chào tạm biệt nhau nên đáp án đúng là 1.
explain.en:
1. Goodbye.
2. Good night.
3. I will eat well.
4. Thank you.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person leaving to the person staying)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person staying to the person leaving)

These two sentences are used when saying goodbye, so the correct answer is 1.
explain.it:
1. Arrivederci.
2. Buonanotte.
3. Mangerò bene.
4. Grazie.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g>Arrivederci (detto dalla persona che parte alla persona che resta)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g>Arrivederci (detto dalla persona che resta alla persona che parte)

Queste due frasi si usano per salutarsi, quindi la risposta corretta è 1.

#######

1. Goodbye.
2. Good night.
3. I will eat well.
4. Thank you.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person leaving to the person staying)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person staying to the person leaving)

These two sentences are used when saying goodbye, so the correct answer is 1.
explain.fr:
1. Au revoir.
2. Bonne nuit.
3. Je mangerai bien.
4. Merci.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g>Au revoir (dit par la personne qui part à la personne qui reste)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g>Au revoir (dit par la personne qui reste à la personne qui part)

Ces deux phrases sont utilisées pour dire au revoir, la bonne réponse est donc 1.

#######

1. Goodbye.
2. Good night.
3. I will eat well.
4. Thank you.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person leaving to the person staying)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person staying to the person leaving)

These two sentences are used when saying goodbye, so the correct answer is 1.
explain.es:
1. Adiós.
2. Buenas noches.
3. Comeré bien.
4. Gracias.
--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g>Adiós (dicho por la persona que se va a la persona que se queda)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g>Adiós (dicho por la persona que se queda a la persona que se va)

Estas dos frases se utilizan al decir adiós, por lo que la respuesta correcta es 1.

#######

1. Goodbye.

--------------------
<g>안녕히 계세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person leaving to the person staying)
<g>안녕히 가세요:</g> Goodbye (said by the person staying to the person leaving)

These two sentences are used when saying goodbye, so the correct answer is 1.

title: [5～6] 다음을 듣고 <보기>와 같이 다음 말에 이어지는 것을 고르십시오.

g_text_audio_translate.vi:
가: Ở lại mạnh khỏe nhé/Tạm biệt.
나: ___________________________
g_text_audio_translate.id:
<g>가:</g>Tetap sehat/Selamat tinggal.
<g>나:</g>______________

#######

<g>가:</g> Stay healthy/Goodbye.
<g>나:</g> ___________________________

explain.vi:
1. Xin mời vào. 
2. Lâu rồi không gặp.
3. Đi cẩn thận nhé/Tạm biệt.
4. Của anh/chị đây.
--------------------
Cả 안녕히 계세요 và 안녕히 가세요 đều có thể dịch là tạm biệt, tuy nhiên 안녕히 가세요 là lời người ở lại nói, còn 안녕히 계세요 là lời người đi nói.
explain.en:
1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
Both <g>안녕히 계세요</g> and <g>안녕히 가세요</g> can be translated as goodbye; however, <g>안녕히 가세요</g> is the saying of person who stays, while <g>안녕히 계세요</g> is the saying of person who leaves.
explain.it:
1. Benvenuto.
2. È da molto tempo che non ci vediamo.
3. Guida con prudenza/Arrivederci.
4. Ecco qua.
--------------------
Entrambi<g>안녕히 계세요</g>E<g>안녕히 가세요</g>può essere tradotto come arrivederci; Tuttavia,<g>안녕히 가세요</g>è il detto di persona che resta, mentre<g>안녕히 계세요</g>è il detto di chi se ne va.

#######

1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
Both <g>안녕히 계세요</g> and <g>안녕히 가세요</g> can be translated as goodbye; however, <g>안녕히 가세요</g> is the saying of person who stays, while <g>안녕히 계세요</g> is the saying of person who leaves.
explain.fr:
1. Bienvenue.
2. Ça fait longtemps que je ne vois pas.
3. Conduisez prudemment/Au revoir.
4. Vous y êtes.
--------------------
Les deux<g>안녕히 계세요</g>et<g>안녕히 가세요</g>peut être traduit par au revoir; cependant,<g>안녕히 가세요</g>est le dicton de celui qui reste, tandis que<g>안녕히 계세요</g>c'est le dicton de celui qui part.

#######

1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
Both <g>안녕히 계세요</g> and <g>안녕히 가세요</g> can be translated as goodbye; however, <g>안녕히 가세요</g> is the saying of person who stays, while <g>안녕히 계세요</g> is the saying of person who leaves.
explain.es:
1. Bienvenido.
2. Mucho tiempo sin verte.
3. Conduce con cuidado/Adiós.
4. Aquí tienes.
--------------------
Ambos<g>안녕히 계세요</g>y<g>안녕히 가세요</g>puede traducirse como adiós; sin embargo,<g>안녕히 가세요</g>es el dicho de persona que se queda, mientras<g>안녕히 계세요</g>es el dicho de persona que se va.

#######

1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
Both <g>안녕히 계세요</g> and <g>안녕히 가세요</g> can be translated as goodbye; however, <g>안녕히 가세요</g> is the saying of person who stays, while <g>안녕히 계세요</g> is the saying of person who leaves.
explain.id:
1. Selamat datang.
2. Lama tidak bertemu.
3. Berkendara dengan hati-hati/Selamat tinggal.
4. Ini dia.
--------------------
Keduanya<g>안녕히 계세요</g>Dan<g>안녕히 가세요</g>dapat diterjemahkan sebagai selamat tinggal; Namun,<g>안녕히 가세요</g>adalah pepatah orang yang tinggal sementara<g>안녕히 계세요</g>itulah pepatah orang yang pergi.

#######

1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
Both <g>안녕히 계세요</g> and <g>안녕히 가세요</g> can be translated as goodbye; however, <g>안녕히 가세요</g> is the saying of person who stays, while <g>안녕히 계세요</g> is the saying of person who leaves.
explain.ja:
1. ようこそ。
2. お久しぶりです。
3. 慎重に運転してください/さようなら。
4. どうぞ。
--------------------
両方<g>안녕히 계세요</g>そして<g>안녕히 가세요</g>「さようなら」と翻訳できます。しかし、<g>안녕히 가세요</g>は留まる人の言葉ですが、<g>안녕히 계세요</g>去る者の名言です。

#######

1. Welcome.
2. Long time no see.
3. Drive carefully/Goodbye.
4. Here you are.
--------------------
